mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate dropping below 5.5% signals labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending and corporate profit margins, generating positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative or flat in >55% of months where UNRATE crosses below 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in real estate sales and housing starts indicate synchronized demand across the housing sector, which amplifies construction and financial sector earnings over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month industrial production growth during an established expansion phase reflects robust manufacturing output that translates into immediate short-term equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with INDPRO momentum during expansion periods, or the signal decays to Rank IC < 0.01 within 18 months.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 2.5% annually triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets post positive 20-day returns in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5%, or the negative signal is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly nonfarm payroll additions reflect sustained economic activity and wage growth, which directly supports corporate revenue expansion and generates positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no consistent positive association with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 for consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between sluggish quarterly GDP growth and declining real estate sales signals weakening aggregate demand that precedes earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 2.0% AND RSAFS monthly decline
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Tight labor markets coinciding with stagnant industrial production indicate supply-side bottlenecks that constrain output growth and trigger negative equity returns over the next quarter during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where both conditions are met, or the joint signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.03) when tested on post-2015 data.
